prompt: A connectivity monitor is shared by route-scoped observers. During ordinary single-route use, live transitions arrive in order and the platform binding closes when the route leaves. During a route handoff, however, the entering route can stay blank until the next radio change; removing the old route sometimes closes the binding under the new route; and after a suspend/resume cycle a callback already queued by the retired platform binding can overwrite the resumed status. Two observers attached before the first event both receive it, and no issue appears when the final observer is removed without later resuming. Platform callbacks can already be queued when close is called, owner names are unique while attached, and the monitor must keep a single shared binding while any owner remains. Keep the source API synchronous and deterministic.
